name: New Era Debt Solutions
description_short: New Era Debt Solutions is a performance-based debt settlement company that negotiates directly with creditors to reduce unsecured debt balances, charging fees only after a settlement is reached.
description_long:
New Era Debt Solutions is a debt settlement company founded in 1999 and headquartered in Camarillo, California. With more than 25 years in operation, the firm has settled over $275 million in client debt, making it one of the more experienced players in the debt relief industry. The company holds an A+ rating with the Better Business Bureau, where it has been accredited since 2001, and is a member of the International Association of Professional Debt Arbitrators (IAPDA). Its near-perfect consumer review scores — 4.95/5 on BBB, 5/5 on Trustpilot, and 4.9/5 on Google — reflect a consistent track record of client satisfaction spanning more than two decades.

New Era's service is built around direct creditor negotiation. Clients enroll eligible unsecured debts — typically credit cards, department store cards, personal loans, revolving charge accounts, and some medical bills — and funds are accumulated in a dedicated savings account over time. As balances grow, New Era's negotiators contact each creditor to settle accounts for less than the full amount owed. A dedicated debt specialist is assigned to each client and manages the entire process, communicating updates and securing client approval before any settlement is finalized. The company reports an average program completion time of 28 months and average savings of approximately 47.77% off the enrolled balance before fees are applied.

What distinguishes New Era from many competitors is its strictly performance-based fee structure. The company charges between 14% and 25% of the enrolled debt amount — but only after a settlement is reached and approved by the client. There are no upfront fees and no monthly service charges. This model removes significant financial risk for clients entering the program, since New Era earns nothing unless it delivers a result. The fee range is at or below the industry average of 15%–25%, and the company's IAPDA accreditation signals a commitment to professional standards in an industry that has historically attracted bad actors.

Despite its strong reputation, New Era has meaningful limitations prospective clients should weigh carefully. Fee percentages are not disclosed on the company's website — clients must call for a personalized quote, reducing upfront pricing transparency. Like all debt settlement programs, clients should expect their credit scores to decline materially during enrollment, as accounts are typically allowed to become delinquent before creditors agree to negotiate. Settled debt amounts may also be reported to the IRS as taxable income via Form 1099-C. The company does not operate in Iowa, Maine, or Oregon, and a past regulatory action in Oregon — resulting in a $50,000 penalty for operating without proper state registration — is a relevant detail for consumers evaluating the firm's compliance history.

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee confirmed. Conflicting sources exist — one customer testimonial references a guarantee, but at least one review site explicitly states it is not available and the company website does not feature guarantee terms. Treat as unavailable unless confirmed directly with the company.

tiers.name: Performance-Based Settlement
features: Free initial debt analysis and consultation
Dedicated debt settlement specialist assigned throughout the program
Direct creditor negotiation to reduce principal balances on enrolled accounts
Client approval required before any settlement is finalized
Fee of 14%–25% of enrolled debt charged only after successful settlement
No upfront fees and no monthly service charges
Covers credit cards, personal loans, medical bills, and other unsecured debts

pros: Performance-based fees only — no charges until a settlement is reached and client-approved
Fee range of 14%–25% is at or below the industry average of 15%–25%
A+ BBB rating with accreditation since 2001 and only one resolved complaint on record
Near-perfect review scores: 4.95/5 BBB, 5/5 Trustpilot, 4.9/5 Google
97% client satisfaction rate per company-reported survey
Dedicated debt specialist assigned to each client throughout the program
$275 million in total debt settled since founding in 1999
cons: Fee percentage not disclosed on the website — clients must call for a personalized quote
Credit scores typically drop significantly during enrollment as accounts become delinquent
Settled debt may be reported to the IRS as taxable income via Form 1099-C
Does not serve Iowa, Maine, or Oregon; past regulatory action in Oregon resulted in a $50,000 penalty
No Sunday customer support — service limited to Monday–Saturday, 7am–8pm PT
best_for: Consumers with $10,000 or more in unsecured debt who cannot afford minimum payments
People with multiple credit card or personal loan balances seeking negotiated principal reductions
Clients who want a strictly performance-based fee model with zero upfront costs
Those who value an experienced, IAPDA-accredited firm with a 25-year track record
diagnosis: Best suited for consumers with $10,000+ in unsecured debt experiencing genuine financial hardship who want zero upfront risk. Be aware that credit scores will drop during enrollment and settled debt may be taxable as income.
services: Free initial debt consultation and financial analysis
Creditor negotiation to reduce principal balances on unsecured accounts
Credit card and revolving charge account debt settlement
Unsecured line of credit debt resolution
Dedicated debt specialist support throughout the program
typical_results_timeline: The program typically runs 24–48 months, with a company-reported average completion time of 28 months. Individual account settlements may be reached earlier; the company reports average savings of approximately 47.77% off enrolled balances before fees are deducted.
